text:
**Hook context is Trellis-derived, but not live-graph authoritative in the same sense.** The reconciler correctly models the fabric snapshot as declared inputs → derived `FabricView` → materialized output frame, with cursor and `now` explicit.  But the actual turn-start and turn-check paths instantiate `HookContextReconciler::new()` inside each assembly function.   That means there is no long-lived hook-context graph in the daemon to inspect the way there is for status/subscriptions.
 [...] Bonus confirmation of the critique's sharpest point: the hook_context reconciler is instantiated fresh per render (`turn_context/start.rs:221`, `check.rs:94`) and discarded. There is no live hook graph to inspect. The critique is right; `probe why hook` against "the live graph" is currently impossible.
